chunk_summary: Guidelines for weight-based F-75 prescription and feed volume targets in SAM management focus on metabolic stabilization, precise feeding schedules, and monitoring intake, especially during the initial stabilization phase.
learning_objectives: Understand the role of F-75 in the stabilization phase of SAM management.
Learn how to calculate and prescribe weight-based F-75 feed volumes and frequencies.
Recognize triggers for NG tube feeding and reassessment in cases of poor intake.
Identify adjustments needed for children with severe edema or vomiting.
key_facts: F-75 is a low-sodium, low-protein therapeutic feed used during the stabilization phase of SAM management.
Daily fluid targets are approximately 130 mL/kg/day, reduced to 100 mL/kg/day in cases of severe (+++) edema.
Feeds are initially given every 2 hours, transitioning to 3-4 hourly only when vomiting and diarrhea are controlled.
Minimum acceptable intake is 80% of the daily target; NG top-up is initiated if intake falls below this threshold.
Breastfeeding should continue on demand alongside therapeutic feeds.
RUTF should not be administered via NG tube; liquid therapeutic feeds are used instead.
reference_quote: Use low-sodium, low-protein F-75 (or equivalent starter diet) given day and night in small, frequent feeds. Write orders in executable bedside terms: 'F-75 ___ mL per feed every ___ hours; daily target ___ mL; minimum acceptable intake (80% of target) ___ mL/day.'
